address: Khao Laem Ya-Mu Ko Samet National Park mainland section, Phe, Mueang Rayong, Rayong, Thailand
opening_hours: National-park access and gate hours can change; recheck locally before a sunrise or sunset-focused visit.

description.en: The mainland headland that best explains Rayong through Ban Phe access, coast views, and Koh Samet-facing perspective.
description.nl: De vastelandkaap die Rayong het best uitlegt via Ban Phe, kustuitzichten en zicht richting Koh Samet.

metaDescription.en: Why Khao Laem Ya is the strongest mainland Rayong stop and how to use it before or instead of a Koh Samet transfer.
metaDescription.nl: Waarom Khao Laem Ya de sterkste vastelandstop van Rayong is en hoe je die gebruikt voor of in plaats van een Koh Samet-transfer.

enhanced_description:
Khao Laem Ya is the mainland Rayong stop that keeps the province from collapsing into a Koh Samet-only page. The headland matters because it lets you read the Ban Phe side of the coast, the Samet-facing horizon, and the national-park framing in one visit without boarding a boat first.

That is why it works so well as the opening move of a Rayong trip. Once you stand on the rocks and see how the coast bends around Ban Phe, Rayong starts to feel like a province with a mainland identity, not just a pier.
detailed_history: Tourism Authority of Thailand's current Khao Laem Ya article still frames the site as the calmer mainland side of the Samet route, which is useful because it keeps the national-park headland visible in Rayong's travel logic.
visitor_experience: Expect a coastal headland visit rather than a deep jungle trek. The value here is the combination of sea air, rocky shoreline, and the sense of how Ban Phe and Koh Samet fit together geographically.

best_time_to_visit.crowd_level: Usually easier to enjoy outside the busiest holiday windows

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Best reached by car, driver, or a deliberate Ban Phe-side transfer plan.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Works naturally with Ban Phe, Mae Ramphueng, or a same-day decision about whether Koh Samet should be an extension.
practical_info.food_options: Eat on the Ban Phe or mainland-coast side rather than expecting this to be the meal stop itself.
practical_info.shopping: Use Ban Phe markets for seafood products and local goods rather than relying on the viewpoint area.

insider_tips: This is the strongest first coastal stop in Rayong if you want the province to make sense.
Sunset is the cleanest fit, but recheck park access if you are timing the visit close to closing.
Do not rely on older static fee pages; current park arrangements can change.
verified_note: Source-backed summary based on Tourism Authority of Thailand coverage. Reconfirm current park access, fees, and transport arrangements before travel day.
cultural_significance: Khao Laem Ya matters because it anchors Rayong's mainland identity within the same coastal story that usually gets handed entirely to Koh Samet.

contentSources.name: Khao Laem Ya: The Serene Side of Samet
